content:
4 min read
Just now

--

JavaScript development is entering another period of rapid change.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

AI-assisted development is reshaping engineering workflows. Enterprise teams are modernizing legacy applications while adopting new frameworks. User expectations continue to rise, yet software must remain maintainable, secure, and scalable over the long term.
Keeping pace with these changes requires more than following the latest framework release or reading documentation. It requires learning from practitioners who are solving these challenges in production.
That is the focus of JS Days 2026, Sencha’s free virtual JavaScript conference taking place on September 16–17, 2026.
Bringing together developers, software architects, engineering leaders, and JavaScript practitioners from around the world, the conference explores the technologies, architectural decisions, and development practices shaping modern web applications.
Rather than presenting isolated product demonstrations, the program is designed around the questions engineering teams are already asking.
From AI Experiments to Production Applications
Over the past year, AI has moved beyond prototypes and proof-of-concept projects. Many organizations are now evaluating how AI capabilities can be integrated into production applications without compromising performance, governance, or maintainability.
One of the featured technical sessions, presented by Marc Gusmano, explores how JavaScript, React, and ReExt can be used to build AI-powered applications using practical implementation patterns rather than theoretical examples.
The discussion focuses on architectural considerations, integration approaches, and the realities of incorporating AI into enterprise development workflows.
Designing Applications That Scale Beyond the First Release
Building an application is only the beginning.
As products mature, development teams face increasingly complex architectural decisions around component reuse, framework interoperability, code maintainability, and long-term scalability.
Several sessions throughout JS Days 2026 examine these challenges from different perspectives, including frontend architecture, cross-framework development, and engineering practices that support sustainable application growth.
Rather than focusing solely on new technologies, the conference emphasizes the decisions that continue to matter long after an application reaches production.
Why Enterprise UI Engineering Still Matters
Modern user interfaces are expected to be intuitive, consistent, and responsive across increasingly complex business applications.
Achieving that level of quality requires more than selecting the right framework. It demands thoughtful component design, consistent interaction patterns, accessibility considerations, and reusable UI architecture.
In this session, Rafael Méndez, Sencha MVP, explores the engineering principles behind enterprise-grade user interfaces, highlighting the design patterns and implementation practices that contribute to scalable frontend development.
For teams building business-critical applications, these considerations remain fundamental to delivering software that users can rely on every day.
Learning From Production Experience
Some of the most valuable lessons in software engineering come from understanding what happened after deployment.
Production case studies provide insights that documentation and tutorials often cannot — revealing architectural trade-offs, implementation challenges, and the practical decisions that shape successful applications.
At JS Days 2026, César Martell presents a detailed walkthrough of a real recruitment application, examining the technical decisions made throughout the development lifecycle and the lessons learned along the way.
For architects, senior developers, and engineering leaders, these experiences often provide valuable context for similar decisions within their own organizations.
A Conference Designed Around Technical Conversations
Technical conferences are most valuable when they create opportunities for discussion, not just presentations.
In addition to expert-led sessions, JS Days 2026 includes live Q&A sessions and interactive discussions where attendees can engage directly with speakers, explore implementation challenges, and exchange ideas with other members of the JavaScript community.
These conversations often extend beyond individual technologies to address broader engineering topics such as architecture, maintainability, performance, and modern development workflows.
More Than a Conference Agenda
The individual sessions at JS Days 2026 cover a wide range of technologies and engineering disciplines, but together they reflect a broader objective.
They address the practical challenges facing today’s JavaScript teams — from integrating AI responsibly and modernizing application architectures to building maintainable user interfaces and learning from real production experience.
Whether you’re an experienced software architect, a frontend engineer, or a developer responsible for enterprise applications, the program is designed to provide technical insights that remain relevant long after the conference concludes.
Event Details
JS Days 2026
Dates: September 16–17, 2026
Format: Fully virtual
Cost: Free
Registration requires only a short online form at jsdays.io. Once registered, attendees will receive agenda updates, speaker announcements, and event access information ahead of the conference.
About Sencha
JS Days 2026 is organized by Sencha, part of Idera, Inc., the team behind Ext JS, ReExt, and GXT. For decades, Sencha has helped organizations build secure, scalable, enterprise-grade JavaScript applications across industries including financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, government, and enterprise software. Today, Sencha technologies are trusted by more than 2 million developers and 150,000+ organizations worldwide.
